The Institute of Cheating Before the Finish Line credential counter
workshop_pitch: You selected Monstrous Other plus Collapse Prophet and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is infection. Their primary moral texture is performative betrayal. Their exposure lateral is Sacramental Test Environments. Their core competency stack is Contamination Authority. Their sidekick is a compliance lieutenant who stays because they are debt-knotted. Their formal training comes from The Institute of Cheating Before the Finish Line.
opening_image: Something wet, living, or almost living has already crossed the category boundary.
dossier_summary: This build runs on appetite inside a nest. At the story level, it violates the body or category boundary itself. The primary moral texture is performative betrayal. The internal lie is simple: bodies are doors. The exposure profile is sacramental test environments, which operationalized into contamination authority. The local ecosystem includes a compliance lieutenant who handles domestic normalization. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is posting private DMs for public credibility.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of performative betrayal shaped by sacramental test environments and confident enough to treat body-boundary indifference like a normal leadership behavior. The compliance lieutenant nearby makes the whole thing feel alarmingly maintainable.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to posting private DMs for public credibility. Their current strengths remain body-boundary indifference, ritualized exposure, appetite governance. The compliance lieutenant remains responsible for domestic normalization and is currently debt-knotted. Do not mention the Council for Schemes That Backfire in the Final Reel matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the nest where infection briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: welcomes disaster because it proves the worldview. The compliance lieutenant quietly handles domestic normalization in the background, which is why the whole thing almost works. Somewhere in the room, somebody realizes too late that the sidekick is terrifyingly competent and never folds cleanly.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one monstrous other build will learn that harm can be limited but not fully undone. Exposure: Sacramental Test Environments. Core competencies: body-boundary indifference, ritualized exposure. Sidekick: compliance lieutenant on domestic normalization. Somebody is definitely posting private DMs for public credibility in act one and that should have been the warning.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for posting private DMs for public credibility. So does the sidekick ecosystem: another compliance lieutenant arrives, still debt-knotted, still handling domestic normalization.
sample_lines: If posting private DMs for public credibility bothers you, wait until you meet my compliance lieutenant.
My core competencies include body-boundary indifference, and my compliance lieutenant handles domestic normalization.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: The villain is less interested in victory than in teaching the room a worse definition of normal.
Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be posting private DMs for public credibility.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn even the compliance lieutenant is hanging around because they are debt-knotted.
